mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising residential housing starts signals broad-based economic acceleration, which compresses equity risk premiums and lifts forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero correlation with 20-day SPX returns in walk-forward testing, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising core inflation compresses real yields and forces equity investors to price in higher nominal growth, lifting short-term market returns when the policy rate remains near zero.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.2% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts reflects improving consumer confidence and credit availability, which mechanically supports forward equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between HOUST month-over-month changes and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth exceeding 200,000 per month validates economic expansion, reducing recession risk premia and driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of months where payroll growth exceeds 200,000.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production outpaces real personal consumption expenditures, supply-side efficiency gains expand corporate margins, directly boosting forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and RSAFS growth rates shows no predictive power for 60-day forward returns, or produces a negative sign consistently.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth signals robust aggregate demand, which lifts equity valuations over the next month, but only when the unemployment rate remains above 5.5% to avoid wage-inflation fears.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns decline when GDPC1 growth accelerates while UNRATE drops below 5.5%, indicating the mechanism fails under tight labor conditions.
